more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Fuel prices surge in Zimbabwe
more.description: Zimbabweans are facing rising fuel costs following fresh price hikes announced by the Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority, as global crude oil markets tighten due to ongoing conflict in the Middle East. In a statement released on Wednesday, ZERA confirmed that diesel prices have jumped from US$1.77 to US$2.05 per litre, while blended petrol has risen [...] The post Fuel prices surge in Zimbabwe appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: “Once a soldier, always a soldier”: Retired generals issue stern warning to Mliswa
more.description: HARARE - A group of retired generals led by Air Marshal (Retired) Henry Muchena has warned former Norton legislator Temba Mliswa over his remarks on their submission to Parliament regarding Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3, insisting their actions were constitutional and reaffirming their stance with the declaration that “once a soldier, always a soldier.” The post “Once a soldier, always a soldier”: Retired generals issue stern warning to Mliswa appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Tungwarara Faces Backlash Over Controversial River Rehabilitation Project in Mazowe
more.description: MAZOWE – Paul Tungwarara, investment adviser to President Emmerson Mnangagwa and chairperson of Prevail Group International, is facing growing resistance from landowners in Mazowe over a controversial riverbed mining rehabilitation project. The dispute centres on the government-backed “Presidential River Rehabilitation Programme,” for which Tungwarara’s company was awarded an exclusive contract. more.title: Muvevi’s trial finally kicks off, former detective says he was being haunted by goblins when he killed four people
more.description: The trial of murder-accused former cop Jaison Muvevi has finally commenced before High Court judge Justice Gibson Mandaza. The former Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) detective made headlines three years ago after he shot and killed four people in movie-style attacks, leaving four others for dead. more.title: Arrest warrant issued for Blessed Mhlanga
more.description: A warrant of arrest has been issued for Zimbabwean journalist Blessed Mhlanga after he failed to appear before the Harare Magistrates’ Court for the continuation of his trial. The warrant was issued on March 9 by magistrate Sheunesu Matova, following Mhlanga’s absence from scheduled proceedings. His legal team has attributed the failure to attend court [...] The post Arrest warrant issued for Blessed Mhlanga appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .
